title: Corpus Linguistics, Collocations and Heritage Talks at the IDS Institute
content:
Blog post by Elisa Squadrito

Thanks to a CLARIN Mobility Grant, from 27 April to 5 May 2026 I had the opportunity to visit the Leibniz-Institut für Deutsche Sprache (IDS) in Mannheim and discuss my PhD project with experts from the Digital Linguistics Department. Focused on the specialised language of policy documents dealing with the impact of climate change on cultural and natural heritage, my project involves leveraging corpus-based methods to investigate how international organisations frame climate change in relation to Cultural Heritage. To this end, I compiled climaHER, a corpus of policy documents addressing the relationship between climate change and heritage. More specifically, it questions how key terms such as risk, adaptation, and mitigation may shift when they are used in heritage-related policy discourse rather than in broader climate-policy contexts.
creator: Elisa Gorgaini
sourceDescription: Common Language Resources and Technology Infrastructure

title: Tour de CLARIN: Interview with Filip Zeman
content:
Filip Zeman’s PhD project investigates a new corpus-based method for teaching Spanish vocabulary to learners of Spanish as a foreign language. His proposed method uses CLARIN-available corpus query tools and resources and examines their potential for pedagogical applications. He was chosen to represent the CLARIAH-ES consortium because of his commitment to promoting the use of digital humanities infrastructures in language education. The project is highly relevant to the CLARIN community as it contributes to the dissemination of CLARIN-available tools and resources among language teachers and aims to make their use more accessible by producing practical learning resources. His work shows how CLARIN can support both research and educational practice.

German Rigau, CLARIN National Coordinator for Spain

This conversation was led by Laura Gusan

title: Tour de CLARIN: Interview with Guna Rābante-Buša
content:
Guna Rābante-Buša was selected to represent the CLARIN-LV consortium because her research and other activities closely align with CLARIN's goals. As a linguistics PhD student specialising in phonetics, she has not only conducted extensive research using Latvian speech corpora but has also actively contributed to the development of several recent speech datasets, which are registered in the CLARIN-LV repository. Guna also actively participates in teaching activities and the development of new course materials, where she actively promotes CLARIN-LV speech resources. Finally, she has also provided valuable support in organising several CLARIN-LV events, including the recent CLARIN and DARIAH Spring conference.
 

Inguna Skadiņa, CLARIN National Coordinator for Latvia

This conversation was led by Laura Gusan
